deck_goal: Recommend a grounded China EV market-entry plan for Europe and Southeast Asia.
audience: Regional strategy leaders deciding 2025 market sequencing

slides.title: China EV Market Entry: Europe & Southeast Asia Strategy
slides.goal: Introduce the strategic recommendation for entering the China EV market in Europe and Southeast Asia, targeting 2025 sequencing decisions.
key_points: Recommend a grounded China EV market-entry plan for Europe and Southeast Asia.
Audience: Regional strategy leaders deciding 2025 market sequencing
slides.speaker_notes: Welcome the audience and outline the purpose of the deck.

slides.title: 出口时间线 (Export Timeline)
slides.goal: Present the evolution of China EV export timeline from 2022 to 2024.
key_points: 2023: Europe and Southeast Asia as dual mainlines
slides.speaker_notes: Explain the transition from single-point shipments to a 分区域深化 approach.

slides.title: 欧洲 vs 东南亚对比 (Europe vs Southeast Asia Comparison)
slides.goal: Compare market characteristics of Europe and Southeast Asia for EV entry.
key_points: Europe: higher revenue per unit, heavier certification, customs, and after-sales compliance
Southeast Asia: faster channel establishment, flexible local partners
Europe suits brand elevation and high ASP; SE Asia suits speed and channel replication
visual_elements: Two-column comparison table: Europe vs SE Asia
slides.speaker_notes: Highlight trade-offs between high ASP and regulatory burden versus speed and flexibility.

slides.title: 进入路径 (Entry Path)
slides.goal: Recommend a phased entry path starting with Southeast Asia then Europe.
key_points: Step 1: Build distribution, service, and spare parts capability in Southeast Asia
Step 2: Validate the operational model in the region
Step 3: Transfer the validated model to Europe key markets
visual_elements: Flowchart showing SE Asia build → validate → Europe expand
slides.speaker_notes: Emphasize risk mitigation by establishing a proven playbook in SE Asia before tackling Europe.

slides.title: 成本与利润指标 (Cost & Margin Indicators)
slides.goal: Show battery cost reduction and its impact on pricing and channel investment.
key_points: Battery pack cost down 18% over the past 12 months
Provides headroom for competitive pricing, channel subsidies, and after-sales investment
visual_elements: Buffer zones marked for pricing, subsidies, and after-sales
slides.speaker_notes: Discuss how this cost reduction enables more aggressive pricing and channel support.

slides.title: Decision Backbone
slides.goal: Summarize the minimum grounded claims supporting the recommended market entry sequence.
key_points: Europe for brand elevation and high ASP; SE Asia for speed and channel replication
18% battery cost reduction creates margin buffer for pricing and channel investment
slides.speaker_notes: Recap the core decisions and their supporting evidence.
